title: Engineering headcount declined 5% this quarter
description: Engineering department has seen 2 resignations and only 1 hire in Q4 2023. Headcount dropped from 18 to 17.
suggestedAction: Review compensation and retention strategies for Engineering team. Consider accelerated hiring for open positions.

title: Unusual spike in absences on Mondays
description: Monday absenteeism rate is 35% higher than other weekdays in November. 8 employees have been consistently absent on Mondays.
suggestedAction: Investigate potential burnout or engagement issues. Conduct stay interviews with affected employees.

title: Payroll costs projected to increase 8% next quarter
description: Based on upcoming regularization of 5 probationary employees and scheduled salary reviews, Q1 2024 payroll is projected at ₱4.2M (up from ₱3.9M).
suggestedAction: Review budget allocations. Consider phasing salary adjustments. Prepare budget presentation for management.

title: Benefits utilization rate below industry average
description: Only 35% of employees used their HMO outpatient benefits this year. Industry average is 55-60%.
suggestedAction: Conduct benefits awareness campaign. Consider wellness programs to increase engagement.

title: Time-to-hire improved by 15% this quarter
description: Average time-to-fill decreased from 33 days to 28 days. Technical roles showing fastest improvement.
suggestedAction: Document successful practices. Consider expanding successful sourcing channels.

title: Overtime costs increased 22% in Engineering
description: Engineering department overtime costs jumped from ₱45K to ₱55K month-over-month. 4 employees exceeded 20 hours OT.
suggestedAction: Review workload distribution. Consider temporary headcount augmentation or project timeline adjustment.

title: 3 high-performers at risk of turnover
description: Analysis of engagement indicators (attendance patterns, performance trends, benefit utilization) suggests emp003, emp015, and emp041 may be at risk.
suggestedAction: Schedule retention conversations. Review compensation competitiveness. Discuss career development plans.

title: Rating calibration variance detected in Sales department
description: Sales department shows 28% higher average ratings compared to company average. Possible rating inflation.
suggestedAction: Schedule calibration session with Sales managers. Review rating guidelines and provide consistency training.

title: 2 compliance deadlines approaching within 7 days
description: 13th Month Pay and Pag-IBIG MCRF filing both due within the next week. Historical compliance is 100%.
suggestedAction: Prioritize these filings. Assign backup approver in case primary is unavailable.

title: Travel expenses 40% below budget this month
description: November travel expenses are significantly under budget. Only ₱52K spent vs ₱150K budget.
suggestedAction: Consider reallocating budget to other categories. Verify if travel restrictions or seasonal factors are contributing.

title: December attendance projected at 88%
description: Historical data shows December attendance drops 5-7% due to holiday season. Projected attendance: 88% vs 93% annual average.
suggestedAction: Plan staffing coverage for critical functions. Communicate holiday schedule expectations early.

title: 5 employees approaching 5-year service milestone
description: emp007, emp010, emp018, emp022, emp036 will reach 5-year tenure within Q1 2024.
suggestedAction: Prepare service award recognition. Review compensation for market competitiveness at 5-year mark.

title: Referral program driving 25% of new hires
description: Employee referrals accounted for 25% of successful hires in 2023, up from 15% last year. Quality scores are higher for referral candidates.
suggestedAction: Consider increasing referral bonus. Promote referral program more actively.

title: Loan applications increased 40% in November
description: Unusual increase in loan applications, particularly company loans. 6 new applications vs average 2-3 per month.
suggestedAction: Review if there are systemic financial stress factors. Consider financial wellness program or advance 13th month processing.
